proven.claim: Correct behaviour: if a title is in `seen` then `skipped` must increase by one.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; LINE: 158
; Correct behaviour: if a title is in `seen` then `skipped` must increase by one.
; Prove that any execution respecting the contract cannot leave `skipped`
; unchanged when a duplicate is detected.
(declare-const title Int)
(declare-const seen_before (Array Int Bool))
(declare-const seen_after (Array Int Bool))
(declare-const skipped_before Int)
(declare-const skipped_after Int)
(declare-const issueExists_ret Bool)

; pre‑condition: title already seen
(assert (= (select seen_before title) true))
(assert (>= skipped_before 0))

; the contract of `issueExists` is that it returns true exactly when the title
; is in the repository.  For this proof we *assume* the contract holds:
(assert (= issueExists_ret true))

; transition: duplicate branch executes → skipped increments, title added to `seen`
(assert (= skipped_after (+ skipped_before 1)))
(assert (= (store seen_before title true) seen_after))

; negated property: skipped does NOT increase despite duplicate detection
(assert (not (= skipped_after (+ skipped_before 1))))

(check-sat)

proven.claim: The log statement itself does not modify state, but the *presence* of the log
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; LINE: 158 (duplicate‑skip logging)
; The log statement itself does not modify state, but the *presence* of the log
; implies that the duplicate branch was taken.  We therefore prove that the
; log can only be emitted when `skipped` has been incremented.
(declare-const issueExists_ret Bool)
(declare-const skipped_before Int)
(declare-const skipped_after Int)

; contract: duplicate branch is entered only if issueExists returns true
(assert (= issueExists_ret true))

; transition: duplicate branch increments `skipped`
(assert (= skipped_after (+ skipped_before 1)))

; negated property: the log is printed while `skipped` did NOT change
(assert (and (= issueExists_ret true) (not (= skipped_after (+ skipped_before 1)))))

(check-sat)

violations.claim: When `issueExists(issue.title)` returns true the code executes the *duplicate* branch,
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; LINE: 158
; When `issueExists(issue.title)` returns true the code executes the *duplicate* branch,
; increments `skipped` and adds the title to `seen`.  The invariant we want is:
; *if a title has been seen before, the only way `skipped` can increase is because
;  issueExists returned true*.
; We show a reachable violation where `issueExists` returns false even though the title
; is already in `seen`, causing a duplicate to be filed instead of skipped.
(declare-const title Int)               ; abstract identifier for the issue title
(declare-const seen_before (Array Int Bool)) ; modeling the Set<string> as a map Int→Bool
(declare-const seen_after (Array Int Bool))
(declare-const skipped_before Int)
(declare-const skipped_after Int)
(declare-const issueExists_ret Bool)

; pre‑condition: the title is already in the seen set
(assert (= (select seen_before title) true))
; counters before processing this issue
(assert (= skipped_before 0))

; transition of the duplicate‑check branch:
;   if issueExists(title) then skip, else fall through.
; We model the buggy situation where issueExists incorrectly returns false.
(assert (= issueExists_ret false))

; because issueExists_ret is false, the code does NOT execute the
;   `skipped++` branch.  Instead it proceeds to the normal filing path,
;   leaving `skipped` unchanged.
(assert (= skipped_after skipped_before))

; Violation condition we want to demonstrate:
; *the title was already seen* ∧ *skipped was NOT incremented*.
(assert (and (= (select seen_before title) true)
             (= skipped_after skipped_before)))

(check-sat)
